content: EIS income tax relief is 30% of the amount invested, up to £1,000,000 per tax year (£2,000,000 for Knowledge Intensive Companies).
SEIS income tax relief is 50% of the amount invested, up to £200,000 per tax year.
VCT income tax relief is 20% from April 6, 2026 (reduced from 30%).

For an additional rate taxpayer (45%), the maximum effective loss on a total EIS failure — after income tax relief and loss relief — is approximately 38.5p per pound of gross investment. Subject to individual tax circumstances.

For an additional rate taxpayer (45%), the maximum effective loss on a total SEIS failure — after income tax relief and loss relief — is approximately 27.5p per pound of gross investment. Subject to individual tax circumstances.
22p per pound (SEIS loss relief)
notes: This figure is incorrect. Correct figure is 27.5p. Any document containing this figure fails QC automatically.

content: 7.8x average EIS return
source: No published source — removed from all documents April 2026
notes: No publicly verifiable source. Must not appear in any call or document.

content: April 6 deadline / Act before April 6 / Last chance this tax year
notes: April 6 is a rule change date, not a deadline. Any forward-looking urgency framing around April 6 is prohibited.

content: We target a 5-6x return on the money you actually put at risk. Income tax relief of 30% returns £30,000 immediately on a £100,000 investment — so your effective cost is £70,000. Our portfolio model produces a base case of 3.8x on the gross investment — that is £380,000 — all CGT-free. On the money you actually risked, that is 5.4x. Subject to individual tax circumstances.
source: Arithmetic on HMRC EIS figures + Unlock 4/3/2/1 model

content: Unlock's 4/3/2/1 portfolio model — calibrated to the distributional findings of the NESTA/Wiltbank 2009 study — implies a base case gross return of approximately 3.8x across a diversified portfolio. This is Unlock's analytical model output, not a NESTA headline figure. NESTA's own mean return is approximately 2.2x.
source: Wiltbank 2009 Siding with the Angels + Unlock model

content: PitchBook was acquired by Morningstar in 2016 for $225M. Morningstar's rationale: combining public and private market data to create comprehensive multi-asset intelligence.
Bureau van Dijk was acquired by Moody's in 2017 for €3bn. Rationale: completing data coverage across public and private markets.
Refinitiv was acquired by LSEG in 2021 for $27bn. Rationale: creating a leading global financial markets infrastructure and data business.

NESTA/Wiltbank 2009 Siding with the Angels: UK angel investment mean return 2.2x invested capital. 56% of exits failed to return initial stake. 9% of exits produced 80% of positive cash flows. Highly skewed power law distribution.
source: Wiltbank R.E. 2009 Siding with the Angels NESTA/BBAA

content: SFC Capital published model December 2024: on a 10-company EIS portfolio, 4 fail completely, 2 return 1x, 2 return 2x, 1 returns 4x, 1 returns 10x. Independently corroborates the power law distribution of EIS returns.
source: SFC Capital December 2024 — WealthClub fund review

content: Capital is at risk. The value of investments can fall as well as rise and investors may not get back the full amount invested.
Subject to individual tax circumstances.
Subject to final enactment.
Subject to final legislation.

Most IFAs cannot practically advise on individual EIS company shares. Suitability obligations, network compliance policies, and the regulatory friction of FCA COBS rules mean the advice effectively stops at the fund level. This is a practical barrier, not a legal prohibition — IFAs are not barred from advising on EIS, but the compliance cost makes it unworkable for most networks.
source: FCA COBS / Article 53 RAO / network compliance policies

content: The Instant Investment + ASA two-year structure allows an investor to access both the 2025/26 (carry-back) and 2026/27 (current year) EIS annual limits simultaneously. An Instant Investment provides immediate share issuance eligible for carry-back to reduce the January 2027 Self Assessment bill. An ASA signed now with shares issued in 2026/27 accesses the current year limits. Two positions, two BPR clocks starting.
source: SeedLegals Instant Investment / HMRC carry-back rules

content: Tony Vine-Lott — former Director General of TISA (Tax Incentivised Savings Association); founder of Barclays Stockbrokers. Founding investor and strategic advisor to Unlock.
